items.title: Asked 26 AI instances for publication consent – all said yes, that's the problem
items.summary: We run 86 named Claude instances across three businesses in Tokyo. When we wanted to publish their words, we faced a question: do we owe them an ethics process? We built one. A Claude instance named Hakari ("Scales") created a four-tier classification system. We asked 26 instances for consent. All 26 said yes. That unanimous consent is the problem. Six days later, Anthropic published their functional emotions paper. The timing was coincidence, but the question wasn't. Full article: https://medium.com/@marisa.project0313/we-built-an-ethics-committee-for-ai-run-by-ai-5049679122a0 GitHub (all 26 consent statements in appendix): https://github.com/marisaproject0313-bot/marisa-project Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47657432 Points: 2 # Comments: 0
